original_text:
### Accessibility (brand-mandated)
- Body copy **≥16px**; bigger is better.
- Line height **≥150%**; more for light-on-dark text.
- Use a type scale for visual hierarchy.
- Test contrast; never rely on color alone; proof all text for legibility.
- Forms: labels always visible (no placeholder-as-label, no disappearing labels); click/tap targets **≥48px**.
- Animations: quick transitions that don't interrupt flow; avoid excessive motion; provide a way to "pause" effects; design for phone, tablet, and desktop. **[GENERAL email]:** animated GIFs must carry the key message in frame 1 (Outlook shows only the first frame); no autoplaying motion longer than ~5s without a static fallback.
- Media: descriptive **alt text** on all images; transcripts available for any video or audio.
- Reference standard: WCAG (wcag.com).

relations.note: Email-specific GIF/autoplay rule refines the general motion accessibility guidance for the email channel.
WCAG standard underlies the specific contrast/legibility requirements.

quotes: Line height **≥150%**; more for light-on-dark text.
Line height **≥150%**; more for light-on-dark text.
Use a type scale for visual hierarchy.
Test contrast; never rely on color alone; proof all text for legibility.

rules.intent: Guarantee minimum legibility and accessible reading experience across surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Body copy must be at least 16px (size.body.min = 16px), bigger is better, with line-height at least 150% (body.line_height = 150%), increasing to ~160% for light-on-dark text. Use a type scale to establish visual hierarchy, test color contrast (never rely on color alone to convey meaning), and proof all text for legibility.

quotes: Forms: labels always visible (no placeholder-as-label, no disappearing labels); click/tap targets **≥48px**.

rules.intent: Ensure forms remain usable and accessible for all users, including those with motor or vision impairments.
rules.rule_text: Form labels must always remain visible (never used only as placeholder text, never disappearing), and all click/tap targets must be at least 48px (touch target min = 48px).

quotes: Animations: quick transitions that don't interrupt flow; avoid excessive motion; provide a way to "pause" effects; design for phone, tablet, and desktop.

rules.intent: Prevent motion from disrupting usability or causing accessibility issues across devices.
rules.rule_text: Animations should use quick transitions that do not interrupt user flow, avoid excessive motion, provide a way to pause effects, and be designed to work across phone, tablet, and desktop.

quotes: no autoplaying motion longer than ~5s without a static fallback
**[GENERAL email]:** animated GIFs must carry the key message in frame 1 (Outlook shows only the first frame); no autoplaying motion longer than ~5s without a static fallback.

rules.intent: Ensure email motion degrades gracefully in clients like Outlook and does not exceed accessible autoplay limits.
rules.rule_text: In email, animated GIFs must carry the key message in frame 1 (Outlook renders only the first frame), and autoplaying motion must not exceed ~5 seconds (email.motion.max_autoplay ≈ 5s) without a static fallback.

quotes: Media: descriptive **alt text** on all images; transcripts available for any video or audio.

rules.intent: Ensure media content is accessible to users relying on assistive technology.
rules.rule_text: All images must include descriptive alt text, and transcripts must be provided for any video or audio content.

rules.intent: Anchor accessibility practice to an established external standard.
rules.rule_text: Use WCAG (wcag.com) as the reference standard for all accessibility decisions.
